name: Saxony-Anhalt (Germany, 1991-2017)
description: Two equal horizontal stripes, yellow over black.
history: Adopted in 1991 shortly after the re-establishment of the Land of Saxony-Anhalt following German reunification (the state had been dissolved by the East German government in 1952). The yellow and black colours derive from the historic Anhalt and Prussian-Saxon traditions. The plain civil flag was used alongside a state flag bearing the coat of arms. In 2017 the design was revised: the colours and proportions remained, but the coat-of-arms version became the dominant form and the plain bicolour was effectively retired as the primary state representation.

features.symbolism: the historic colours of the Duchy of Anhalt and the Province of Saxony, combined to represent the merged territory
